## Task: Fix [medium] UI/UX: Row menu advertises `aria-haspopup='true'` (menu) but does not implement the menu pattern
## Step: execute

You are a fix agent. Resolve the following final-review finding.

Review dimension: UI/UX
Severity: medium
File: src/app/dom-builders.ts:301-310 (makeRowMenu)
Title: Row menu advertises `aria-haspopup='true'` (menu) but does not implement the menu pattern

Finding:
The per-row ⋮ button sets `aria-haspopup='true'` (which per spec is equivalent to `aria-haspopup='menu'`) and toggles `aria-expanded`, signaling to AT users that activating it opens a menu widget with arrow-key item navigation, Home/End, and focus moving to the first item on open. The actual dropdown is a plain `<div class='row-menu'>` containing `<button class='btn'>` / `<a class='btn'>` items with no `role='menu'`/`role='menuitem'`, no focus move into the menu on open, and no arrow-key handling — only Tab/Shift+Tab and Escape work. This expectation mismatch is confusing for screen-reader users. Either complete the menu semantics or downgrade to a plain disclosure. The lower-risk, self-contained fix is to complete the semantics by adding the roles.

Fix instructions (follow exactly; make targeted, minimal changes):
In src/app/dom-builders.ts `createMenu` (which builds the shared `.row-menu` element), set `menu.setAttribute('role', 'menu')` on the created `menu` div, and set `role='menuitem'` on every element in the passed-in `items` array (iterate and set the attribute, skipping any element that already has a role). Then in `makeRowMenu`'s `openMenu`/click handler, after the menu is shown, focus the first item: add `(items[0] as HTMLElement)?.focus()` is not available here, so instead in `createMenu`'s `openMenu`, after positioning, call `(menu.querySelector('button, a') as HTMLElement | null)?.focus()`. Keep `aria-haspopup='true'` and `aria-expanded` toggling as-is. Verify the toolbar-handlers and render-browse tests still pass (they query buttons by text/className, not by role).

## Review Feedback History (please address all items)
Attempt 1: The menu-pattern fix is implemented correctly in spirit: `role="menu"` is set on the `.row-menu` div, `role="menuitem"` is set on every passed item (skipping items that already carry a role), `aria-haspopup`/`aria-expanded` are left intact, and focus is moved into the menu on open. The dom-builders and toolbar-handlers test suites pass.

However, the change introduces a FAILING test in render-browse.test.ts, violating the task's explicit requirement that 'the toolbar-handlers and render-browse tests still pass.' Root cause: both the production code (`createMenu`'s `openMenu`) and the newly-added test use `menu.querySelector('button, a')` to find the first focusable item. I confirmed via direct experimentation that happy-dom evaluates a comma-separated selector list in SELECTOR-LIST ORDER rather than the spec-mandated DOCUMENT ORDER: on a menu whose children are `[<a Download>, <button Delete>, ...]`, `querySelector('button, a')` returns the first `<button>` (Delete), not the first-in-document `<a>` (Download). (`querySelector('a, button')` returns the `<a>`; `querySelectorAll` IS document-ordered; `firstElementChild` is correct.)

Consequences: (1) In the test environment, opening a FILE row menu focuses the Delete button instead of the Download anchor — the focus-into-menu contract is violated under happy-dom. (2) The new test 'opening the menu moves focus onto the first menu item (file menu → Download <a>)' fails: `expected 'BUTTON' to be 'A'`. Real browsers are spec-compliant so production behaves correctly, but CI is red. Note the folder-menu right-click focus test passes only by coincidence — there the first child is already a `<button>`.

Fix: replace `menu.querySelector('button, a')` with a document-order-robust selection in BOTH the production code and the test. The `items` array is in scope inside `createMenu` (it is the first parameter), so `items[0]?.focus()` directly targets the first menu item in insertion order; alternatively `(menu.firstElementChild as HTMLElement | null)?.focus()` works since every direct child of the menu is an item. The test should likewise select its expected element via `menu.firstElementChild` (or `menu.children[0]`) rather than the fragile comma selector — fixing the production code alone is insufficient because the test independently re-derives its expected element with the same buggy selector and would still fail on `document.activeElement`.
